# @zakkster/lite-headless/steps

Multi-step process indicator. NOT the numeric stepper (that's
`stepper` for +/- on a number). This is the "step 3 of 5" pattern used
in checkout flows, onboarding wizards, multi-page forms, and approval
workflows.

## Per-step status

- **`complete`** — passed; can navigate back to it
- **`current`** — the active step
- **`pending`** — not yet reached
- **`error`** — failed validation; needs attention (explicit override)

Default status is derived from each step's index vs the current index.
`setStepStatus(id, "error")` overrides for that one step.

## API

```js
import { createSteps } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/steps";

const steps = createSteps({
    steps: [
        { id: "account", title: "Account" },
        { id: "billing", title: "Billing" },
        { id: "review",  title: "Review" },
    ],
    defaultCurrent: 0,
    orientation: "horizontal",     // or "vertical"
    allowBack: true,                // can navigate to completed steps
    allowSkip: false,               // can jump forward past current
    onStepChange: (nextIdx, prevIdx, reason) => {},
    onComplete: () => {},           // current === steps.length
});
```

## Reactive accessors

```js
steps.steps()           // StepDef[]
steps.current()         // number (or -1 if no steps, or length if complete)
steps.currentStep()     // StepDef | null
steps.statusOf(idOrIndex)
steps.isComplete()      // current >= steps.length
steps.progress()        // 0..1 fraction
```

## Queries (non-reactive)

```js
steps.getStep(id)
steps.indexOf(id)
steps.canNavigateTo(i)
```

## Mutations

```js
steps.next() / .prev()
steps.setCurrent(i, reason)
steps.setCurrentById(id, reason)
steps.setSteps(arr)              // replaces; clamps current; drops orphan overrides
steps.setStepStatus(id, "error" | null)
```

## Attach

```js
steps.attachRoot(el)             // role=list; data-orientation, data-step-count, data-current-index, data-complete
steps.attachStep(el, id)         // role=listitem; data-status, data-current/error/complete, aria-current, tabindex
steps.attachNextButton(el)       // disables when complete
steps.attachPrevButton(el)       // disables on first step
```

Step elements click-to-navigate when `canNavigateTo()` returns true.
Disabled steps have `tabindex="-1"` so they're skipped by tab order.

## Painted attributes

### Root
| attr                 | when                                 |
|----------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `role`               | `list`                               |
| `aria-label`         | `Progress`                           |
| `data-orientation`   | `horizontal` or `vertical`           |
| `data-step-count`    | number of steps                      |
| `data-current-index` | current index (-1 to length)         |
| `data-complete`      | when isComplete()                    |

### Step elements
| attr             | when                                          |
|------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| `role`           | `listitem`                                    |
| `data-step-id`   | the step id                                   |
| `data-status`    | `complete | current | pending | error`        |
| `data-index`     | step's index                                  |
| `data-navigable` | canNavigateTo() === true                       |
| `data-current`   | when status === "current"                     |
| `data-complete`  | when status === "complete"                    |
| `data-error`     | when status === "error"                       |
| `aria-current`   | `step` (current) or `false`                   |
| `tabindex`       | `0` (navigable) or `-1` (not navigable)       |

## Custom element

```html
<lite-steps orientation="horizontal" current="1">
    <ol>
        <li data-step-id="account">Account</li>
        <li data-step-id="billing">Billing</li>
        <li data-step-id="review">Review</li>
    </ol>
    <div>
        <button data-step-prev>Back</button>
        <button data-step-next>Continue</button>
    </div>
</lite-steps>
```

The wrapper auto-discovers steps from `[data-step-id]` markup and
the prev/next buttons. The `title` field of each step defaults to
the element's textContent.

### Host attributes
- `current="<int>"`
- `orientation="horizontal|vertical"`
- `allow-skip`
- `no-back`

### Host API
```js
host.index / .step / .isComplete / .progress
host.next() / .prev() / .setCurrent(i) / .setCurrentById(id)
host.setStepStatus(id, "error" | null)
host._stepsInstance
```

### Custom element events
- `stepchange` `{ detail: { current, previous, reason } }`
- `complete`

## Composition

- **Wizard**: pair with `dialog` for a modal wizard. Each step's content
  swaps in based on `currentStep().id`.
- **Multi-page form**: bind a `form-field` set per step; on Next, run
  validation, call `setStepStatus(id, "error")` if invalid (the step
  stays current and shows the error indicator); else call `next()`.
- **Vertical timeline**: orientation="vertical" + custom CSS for the
  connecting line between steps.
- **Approval flow**: steps reflect submission status from a server
  poll; mark rejected steps as "error".

## Notes

- `current` can equal `steps.length` -- that's the "complete" terminal
  state. `isComplete()` returns true. Next button auto-disables.
- The primitive doesn't render content per step -- only the indicator.
  Pair with your own slot-swap logic to render per-step UI.
- Status overrides are per-step-id, so re-ordering or removing steps
  preserves the right overrides (orphan overrides are dropped on
  `setSteps`).

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
